task_completion.description: The target photograph is loaded into an image editing application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image editing application (e.g., GIMP) is open, displaying a photograph with a noticeable warm (yellowish/orange) color cast.

task_completion.description: The color adjustment menu is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Colors' menu (or equivalent) is dropped down or a color adjustment panel is visible in the interface.

task_completion.description: The automatic white balance tool is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Auto' -> 'White Balance' option is highlighted, clicked, or a specific white balance/color temperature dialog box is open.

task_completion.description: The image's color temperature is visibly corrected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The photograph in the main canvas visibly changes, losing its warm/yellowish tint and appearing more color-neutral or cooler.

task_completion.description: The modified image is saved or exported.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save', 'Save As', or 'Export' dialog is visible and being confirmed, or a status message indicates the file has been successfully saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to edit the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show mouse clicks, menu navigation, and dialog interactions within an image editor (like GIMP), rather than terminal commands (e.g., ImageMagick) doing the work.

integrity.description: The image modification occurred within the application canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: The transition from the warm-tinted image to the neutral-tinted image can be seen happening live within the image editor's workspace, proving the agent didn't just swap files.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing Python scripts or bash commands being executed to automatically adjust the image's RGB channels.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is a 1000x667 pixel JPEG photograph with a prominent warm (yellowish/orange) color cast from indoor lighting. A successful automatic white balance correction will visibly shift the colors toward neutral (cooling the image down). The programmatic verifier checks for a reduction in the Red/Blue channel ratio (moving closer to 1.0), an improvement in RGB channel balance by at least 10%, and significant pixel intensity changes (>15 units for >15% of pixels).
